core_claim: Dirty Frag chains two Linux kernel page-cache write primitives (xfrm-ESP and RxRPC) to achieve deterministic local root on most distributions. Unlike race-condition exploits, it requires no timing window and has very high success rate. It bypasses the Copy Fail mitigation (algif_aead blacklist).

caption:
Dirty Frag is the successor to Copy Fail. It chains two page-cache write primitives in the Linux kernel: xfrm-ESP (IPSec subsystem) and RxRPC. Together they give deterministic local root on most distributions.

Unlike race-condition exploits, Dirty Frag is a deterministic logic bug. No timing window. Kernel does not panic on failure. Very high success rate. One command root.

The chain covers blind spots: xfrm-ESP needs user namespaces (blocked by Ubuntu AppArmor). RxRPC needs no namespace but rxrpc.ko is not in most distros — except Ubuntu loads it by default. One works where the other fails.

The Copy Fail mitigation (blacklisting algif_aead) does not stop Dirty Frag. It works regardless of that module. CloudLinux advises blocklisting esp4, esp6, and rxrpc. But that breaks IPsec/RxRPC functionality.

Microsoft observed limited in-the-wild exploitation: SSH access, then ELF binary triggers privilege escalation via su, modifies GLPI LDAP auth, accesses sensitive data, wipes PHP sessions.

CVE-2026-43284 (xfrm-ESP) and CVE-2026-43500 (RxRPC) patched in mainline. Ubuntu, Debian, RHEL, Fedora, SUSE, Amazon, AlmaLinux, Rocky, CloudLinux all have advisories.

Dirty Frag proves the bug class is wider than one subsystem. Patch the kernel, not the module.

Have you blocklisted esp4, esp6, and rxrpc on systems that do not need IPsec?

Follow for AI security breakdowns without the fake panic.

[Dirty Frag, Linux kernel, local privilege escalation, Copy Fail, xfrm-ESP, RxRPC, page cache, CVE-2026-43284, CVE-2026-43500, kernel hardening]
comment_prompt: Have you blocklisted esp4, esp6, and rxrpc on systems that do not need IPsec?
alt_text: Title card: two primitives, one chain, deterministic root. Dirty Frag bypasses the Copy Fail mitigation with no race condition and hits Ubuntu, RHEL, Fedora, and openSUSE. Two fractured shards fuse into a single glowing red blade in a dark void.

Dirty Frag extends the Dirty Pipe and Copy Fail bug class, chaining the xfrm-ESP and RxRPC page-cache writes. Reported April 30, embargo broken after a third party published xfrm-ESP details, PoC released. A family tree of kernel bugs with Dirty Frag as the newest red node.

A deterministic logic bug with no race condition required, the kernel does not panic on failure, and the success rate is very high. It hits Ubuntu 24.04.4, RHEL 10.1, openSUSE Tumbleweed, CentOS Stream 10, AlmaLinux 10, and Fedora 44, with Microsoft seeing limited in-the-wild use. A precision mechanism clicks into place every time.

xfrm-ESP gives a four-byte store via IPsec but needs a user namespace that Ubuntu AppArmor blocks. RxRPC needs no namespace but rxrpc.ko is absent on most distros, except Ubuntu loads it by default. Chaining covers both blind spots for one-command root. Two gears marked ESP and RxRPC drive a ROOT output shaft.

The Copy Fail mitigation blacklists algif_aead, but Dirty Frag works regardless because ESP and RxRPC are separate attack surfaces. Blocklisting esp4, esp6, and rxrpc breaks IPsec and RxRPC, so patching is the only real fix. A blocked shield with two red arrows bypassing it.

Blocklist esp4, esp6, and rxrpc if you do not need IPsec or RxRPC, restrict unprivileged user namespaces, harden containers, and monitor for abnormal escalation. CVE-2026-43284 and CVE-2026-43500 are patched in mainline. A split view of a vulnerable kernel beside one with three module slots sealed in purple.

The Copy Fail mitigation does not cover the xfrm or RxRPC paths. Dirty Frag proves the bug class is wider than one subsystem, so patch the kernel, not the module. A kernel source tree with one branch pruned but two others thriving in red.

Closing question: have you blocklisted esp4, esp6, and rxrpc on systems that do not need IPsec? Save this for your next kernel hardening review. A single modprobe.d config line on a clean dark end-card with open negative space.

sources.source: The Hacker News: Linux Kernel Dirty Frag LPE Exploit Enables Root Access
sources.supports: Dirty Frag chains xfrm-ESP and RxRPC page-cache writes; reported April 30; embargo broken; deterministic, no race condition; hits Ubuntu 24.04.4, RHEL 10.1, openSUSE Tumbleweed, CentOS Stream 10, AlmaLinux 10, Fedora 44; xfrm-ESP from 2017 commit, RxRPC from 2023; Ubuntu AppArmor blocks namespace; RxRPC module in Ubuntu by default; Copy Fail mitigation (algif_aead blacklist) does not stop Dirty Frag; CVE-2026-43284 and CVE-2026-43500; CloudLinux blocklist mitigation; Microsoft observed in-the-wild exploitation.

sources.source: Wiz: Dirty Frag Linux Kernel LPE via ESP and RxRPC
sources.supports: Vulnerability chain combining two page-cache write primitives; both allow modification of page-cache-backed memory not exclusively owned by kernel; deterministic and highly reliable like Copy Fail and Dirty Pipe; requires CAP_NET_ADMIN; less likely in hardened containers with seccomp.

sources.source: Microsoft: Active Attack Dirty Frag Linux Vulnerability Expands Post-Compromise Risk
sources.supports: Limited in-the-wild activity; SSH access then ELF binary triggers su privilege escalation; GLPI LDAP auth file modification; reconnaissance; PHP session file wiping.

narration.text: Two primitives. One chain. Deterministic root. Dirty Frag bypasses Copy Fail mitigation.
Chains xfrm-ESP and RxRPC page-cache writes. No race condition. Kernel does not panic on failure. High success rate.
Hits Ubuntu, RHEL, Fedora, openSUSE, AlmaLinux, CentOS Stream. Microsoft saw in-the-wild exploitation.
xfrm-ESP needs namespace (blocked by Ubuntu AppArmor). RxRPC needs no namespace but module not in most distros. Ubuntu loads it.
Chain covers blind spots. One command root. Copy Fail mitigation blacklists algif_aead. Dirty Frag works regardless.
Blocklist esp4, esp6, rxrpc if you do not need IPsec. Restrict namespaces. Harden containers. Patch kernel and reboot.
Copy Fail mitigation does not cover xfrm or RxRPC paths. Dirty Frag proves bug class is wider than one subsystem.
Have you blocklisted esp4, esp6, and rxrpc on systems that do not need IPsec? Tell me in the comments.

beats.caption: Two primitives. One chain. Deterministic root.
Successor to Copy Fail. Deterministic. No race.
Major distros hit. In-the-wild exploitation seen.
ESP needs namespace. RxRPC needs module. Chain covers both.
Copy Fail mitigation bypassed. algif_aead blacklist useless.
Blocklist modules. Restrict namespaces. Patch kernel.
Bug class wider than one subsystem. Patch kernel not module.
Have you blocklisted esp4, esp6, rxrpc?
